info.title: nps.today API
info.description:
Welcome to our Swagger page! Please note that this Swagger page is generated automatically. 
                Here you can find detailed information about the API endpoints, including request and response formats, 
                and try out the endpoints directly from the browser.

get.summary: Retrieves analytics for responses associated with a specific campaign or date range.
get.description:
This endpoint returns analytics data for responses filtered by campaign ID and/or date range.
If a campaign ID is provided, the method checks if the current user has access to the specified campaign.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: Optional. The ID of the campaign to filter responses by.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Optional. The start date of the date range to filter responses by.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Optional. The end date of the date range to filter responses by.

200.description: Returns the analytics data for the specified filters.

400.description: Returned if the specified campaign ID does not belong to the user's organization.

post.summary: Retrieves analytics for multiple campaigns.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the retrieval of analytics data across multiple campaigns. It filters the campaigns based
on the provided list of campaign IDs,
ensuring that only campaigns accessible by the current user's organization are considered. The analytics data is
then aggregated and returned.
This method is useful for generating consolidated analytics reports across multiple campaigns.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Optional. The start date of the date range to filter responses by.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Optional. The end date of the date range to filter responses by.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs to retrieve analytics for.

200.description: Returns the aggregated analytics data for the specified campaigns.

404.description: Returned if no accessible campaigns are found for the current user's organization.

post.summary: Generates charts for responses per organization.
post.description:
This endpoint generates charts based on the analytics data of responses for the specified campaigns and dashboard
configuration.
It filters the campaigns based on the provided list of campaign IDs, ensuring that only those accessible by the
current user's organization are considered.
The method then calculates and returns charts data per organization, which can be used for visual analytics.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the dashboard configuration to use for generating charts.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Optional. The start date of the date range to filter responses by.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Optional. The end date of the date range to filter responses by.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs to generate charts for.

200.description: Returns the charts data per organization for the specified campaigns and dashboard configuration.

post.summary: Retrieves responses per organization based on campaign IDs and dashboard configuration.
post.description:
This endpoint provides analytics per organization for a given set of campaign IDs and a specific dashboard
configuration.
It filters the campaigns to those accessible by the current user's organization and calculates analytics based on
the provided parameters.
The analytics include response counts, averages, and other relevant metrics, sorted by campaign name if specified.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the dashboard configuration to use for analytics.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Optional. The start date of the date range to filter responses by.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Optional. The end date of the date range to filter responses by.

parameters.name: campaignSortName
parameters.description: Optional. Specifies whether to sort the campaigns by name.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs to include in the analytics.

200.description:
Returns the analytics data per organization for the specified campaigns and dashboard
configuration.

get.summary: Retrieves a summary of the organization's data.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a summary of data for the current user's organization, including key metrics and statistics.
The summary provides a quick overview of the organization's performance and activity.

200.description: Returns the organization summary data.

404.description: Returned if the organization summary data is not found.

200.description: OK

get.description: GET /authentication-configuration

200.description: OK

put.description: PUT /authentication-configuration

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of campaign members based on the specified criteria.
get.description:
This endpoint provides access to a paginated list of campaign members, allowing for efficient data retrieval
based on pagination parameters and optional filters such as modification date and first survey exposure date.
It is particularly useful for BI processes that require analysis of campaign member data over specific periods.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return in the result set.

parameters.name: modifiedAfter
parameters.description: Optional. The date after which modified items will be included in the result set.

parameters.name: firstSurveyExposure
parameters.description: Optional. The date of the first survey exposure to filter the campaign members.

200.description: Returns the paginated list of campaign members.

400.description: If the request parameters are invalid.

401.description: If the user is not authorized to access this endpoint.

post.summary: Clears the analytics cache for the current organization.
post.description:
Invokes the analytics cache service to delete cached analytics items. This operation should be used with caution as
it can impact
data retrieval performance until the cache is rebuilt.

200.description: Indicates the cache was successfully cleared.

400.description: Indicates an error occurred while attempting to clear the cache.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of campaigns with support for OData query options.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: OData query options for filtering, sorting, and paging.

requestBody.description: Ordering criteria for analytics data.

200.description: Returns the list of campaigns.

get.description: Retrieves a list of campaigns with support for OData query options.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of campaigns for the current organization.
get.description:
Provides an option to include analytics data and to return a minimal representation of each campaign.
This endpoint requires an employee session token for authorization.

parameters.name: includeAnalytics
parameters.description: Specifies whether to include analytics data in the response.

parameters.name: min
parameters.description: If set to true, returns a minimal representation of the campaigns.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Optional filter to only include active or inactive campaigns.

200.description: Returns the list of campaigns.

post.summary: Creates a new campaign.

requestBody.description: The campaign model to create.

200.description: OK

400.description: If the model is invalid.

post.description: Creates a new campaign.

put.summary: Edits an existing campaign.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign to edit.

requestBody.description: The updated campaign model.

200.description: Returns the updated campaign.

400.description: If the model is invalid.

404.description: If the campaign is not found.

put.description: Edits an existing campaign.

delete.summary: Deletes a specified campaign.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows for the deletion of a campaign by its unique identifier. It requires authorization and is
intended for use by administrators.
The operation is irreversible and will remove all associated data with the campaign.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign to be deleted.

200.description: Indicates the campaign was successfully deleted.

get.summary: Retrieves details of a specific campaign.
get.description:
Provides detailed information about a campaign, identified by its unique identifier. This includes basic campaign
details such as name, description, and status.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

200.description: Returns the campaign details.

post.summary: Clones an existing campaign.
post.description:
This endpoint duplicates a campaign based on the provided campaign ID. It copies all campaign settings,
including survey questions and design elements, but does not copy respondent data.
This operation requires authorization and is intended for use by organization administrators.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign to clone.

200.description: Indicates the campaign was successfully cloned. Returns the success message.

400.description: Indicates an error occurred during the cloning process. Returns the error message.

post.summary: Sends a test email for a specified campaign.
post.description:
This method generates and sends a test email to the specified address using the campaign's template and settings.
It is useful for previewing how the survey will appear to recipients.
If the campaign template is configured to send reminders, a preview reminder email is also sent.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address to send the test email to.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign for which to send a test email.

200.description: Indicates the test email was successfully sent.

400.description: Indicates an error occurred while attempting to send the test email.

post.summary: Adds respondents to a campaign if the provided hash matches the expected value.
post.description:
This method is typically used in conjunction with personalized links or secure invitations. Note: P-GAP is not
applicable for this endpoint.

parameters.name: cid
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign to which respondents are being added.

parameters.name: prefixAndSha
parameters.description:
A string containing the prefix and SHA-256 hash, separated by a '|'. The hash is used for
validation.

requestBody.description: A list of objects representing the respondents to be added.

200.description: Indicates that the respondents were successfully added to the campaign.

409.description: Indicates a conflict, such as when the hash validation fails.

get.summary: Retrieves the template for a given campaign member.
get.description: This endpoint allows for the retrieval of a campaign's template by specifying the campaign member's identifier.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the campaign member whose template is to be retrieved.

200.description: Returns the campaign template.

400.description: Returns an error message if the template could not be retrieved.

post.summary: Resends a survey to a specific campaign member.
post.description:
This endpoint triggers the resend of a survey to a campaign member, identified by their campaign and member IDs.
It supports both email and SMS survey types. The operation requires the campaign member to have a valid email or
SMS record.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign associated with the member.

parameters.name: campaignMemberId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign member to whom the survey will be resent.

200.description: Indicates the survey was successfully resent.

400.description: Indicates the survey could not be resent due to missing email or SMS data.

404.description: Indicates the specified campaign member was not found.

post.summary: Adds a respondent to a specific campaign.
post.description: This endpoint is used to add a single respondent to a campaign. It supports an optional check for P-GAP compliance.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

parameters.name: checkPgap
parameters.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to check for P-GAP compliance.

requestBody.description: The campaign member model representing the respondent to be added.

200.description: Indicates the respondent was successfully added.

400.description: Indicates an error occurred, such as validation failure.

post.summary: Adds a list of respondents to a specified campaign.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the bulk addition of respondents to a campaign. It checks for the ownership of the
campaign,
validates the provided respondent data, and optionally checks for P-GAP compliance if specified. Respondents
failing
validation are not added. This operation supports adding respondents in bulk but can also handle single additions,
especially for P-GAP compliance checks.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign to which respondents are being added.

parameters.name: checkPgap
parameters.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to check for P-GAP compliance for each respondent.

requestBody.description: A list of objects representing the respondents to be added.

200.description: Indicates the respondents were successfully added or provides details on P-GAP requirements.

400.description: Indicates an error occurred, such as validation failure or unauthorized campaign access.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of respondents for a given campaign.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches all respondents associated with a specific campaign. It is useful for obtaining a
comprehensive
list of participants in a campaign for further analysis or action. The operation is subject to the caller's access
permissions
to the campaign data.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

200.description: Returns the list of respondents for the specified campaign.

404.description: Indicates the specified campaign was not found.

post.summary: Checks if an employee is in P-GAP for a specific campaign.
post.description:
This endpoint assesses whether an employee meets the criteria for the  (P-GAP) for a given campaign.
It is used to ensure that only eligible employees are able to participate in certain campaigns, based on predefined
criteria.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

requestBody.description: The model containing the employee's information.

200.description: Returns the P-GAP status of the employee for the specified campaign.

post.summary: Adds a respondent to a campaign with inline authentication.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the addition of a respondent to a specific campaign using inline authentication via an API
key.
It is designed to support scenarios where traditional authentication mechanisms are not feasible, such as
integrations with third-party services like Zendesk.
The API key must be included in the request payload. This method validates the API key, checks campaign ownership,
and processes the respondent data.
Note: P-GAP is not applicable for this endpoint.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign to which the respondent is being added.

requestBody.description: The authentication object containing the API key and respondent information.

200.description: Indicates the respondent was successfully added to the campaign.

400.description: Indicates a bad request, such as invalid data format.

401.description: Indicates unauthorized access due to an invalid or missing API key.

post.summary: Generates survey links for respondents of a given campaign.
post.description:
This endpoint is responsible for generating unique survey links for each respondent of a campaign. These links are
used
by respondents to access and complete the survey. The operation adds respondents to the campaign if they are not
already
part of it and then generates the survey links. Note: P-GAP is not applicable for this endpoint.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

requestBody.description: A list of objects representing the respondents.

200.description: Returns the generated survey links for the respondents.

get.summary: Retrieves the template associated with a specific campaign.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches the template details for a given campaign by its unique identifier. It includes information
such as
notification triggers, attached categories, and language settings. This is useful for understanding the
configuration and
content of the campaign's communications.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

200.description: Returns the template details for the specified campaign.

400.description: Indicates no template was found for the given campaign ID.

404.description: Indicates the template could not be found in the database.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of response categories for a specific campaign.
get.description:
This endpoint provides a list of all response categories associated with a given campaign. Response categories are
used
to classify and organize feedback received from respondents. This information is crucial for analyzing the feedback
in
a structured manner.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

200.description: Returns the list of response categories for the specified campaign.

404.description: Not Found

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of responses across all campaigns.
get.description:
This endpoint provides a way to fetch a paginated list of responses from all campaigns. It supports filtering by
date,
categorization, and other criteria to refine the results. This is useful for analyzing responses across multiple
campaigns.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Filter responses received after this date.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Filter responses received before this date.

parameters.name: empty
parameters.description: Include responses with no feedback content.

parameters.name: uncategorizedOnly
parameters.description: Include only responses that have not been categorized.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order in which to return responses.

parameters.name: includeDoNotContact
parameters.description: Include responses from participants marked 'Do Not Contact'.

post.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of responses from specified campaigns.
post.description:
This endpoint allows fetching a paginated list of responses from a list of specified campaigns, with support for
various filters.
It enables targeted analysis of responses from multiple specific campaigns.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Filter responses received after this date.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Filter responses received before this date.

parameters.name: empty
parameters.description: Include responses with no feedback content.

parameters.name: catOnly
parameters.description: Include only responses that have been categorized.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order in which to return responses.

parameters.name: includeDoNotContact
parameters.description: Include responses from participants marked 'Do Not Contact'.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter responses by participant name.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: Filter responses by external participant ID.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs from which to fetch responses.

200.description: Returns the paginated list of responses from the specified campaigns.

404.description: Returns not found if no campaigns match the specified IDs.

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of responses for a specific campaign.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a paginated list of responses for a given campaign, supporting various filters for
customization.
It's useful for detailed analysis of responses within a single campaign.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Filter responses received after this date.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Filter responses received before this date.

parameters.name: empty
parameters.description: Include responses with no feedback content.

parameters.name: uncategorizedOnly
parameters.description: Include only responses that have not been categorized.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order in which to return responses.

parameters.name: includeDoNotContact
parameters.description: Include responses from participants marked 'Do Not Contact'.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter responses by participant name.

parameters.name: modifiedAfter
parameters.description: Include responses modified after this date.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: Filter responses by external participant ID.

parameters.name: historic
parameters.description: Include historic responses.

200.description: Returns the paginated list of responses for the specified campaign.

put.summary: Edits a response for a given campaign.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the editing of a response associated with a specific campaign. It checks for the existence
of the campaign member
and updates the response based on the provided data. If the response's rating has changed, it schedules alerts
accordingly.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

requestBody.description: The response model containing the updated information.

200.description: Returns the updated response model.

400.description:
Returned when the request content is null or the campaign member does not belong to the specified
campaign.

500.description: Returned when an unexpected error occurs.

get.summary: Retrieves CRM responses with optional modification date filtering.
get.description:
This endpoint provides a paginated list of CRM responses, optionally filtering by the last modification date.
It's designed for integration with CRM systems to fetch responses that have been updated after a certain date.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return in the paginated result.

parameters.name: modifiedAfter
parameters.description: Optional parameter to filter responses modified after this date.

200.description: Returns the paginated list of CRM responses.

get.summary: Retrieves a response by its unique GUID.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a single response based on its globally unique identifier (GUID). It's primarily used for
retrieving
detailed information about a specific response, including respondent details and feedback content. Note: This
method is
marked for removal and will be deprecated in future releases.

parameters.name: cmGuid
parameters.description: The unique GUID of the campaign member's response.

200.description: Returns the response details for the specified GUID.

404.description: Indicates that the response could not be found.

delete.summary: Deletes a campaign member from a campaign.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows for the deletion of a campaign member by their ID from a specific campaign. It checks for the
existence of the campaign member,
deletes them, and updates related analytics caches if necessary. This action is irreversible. This operation
requires the user to have CompanyAdmin role.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

parameters.name: campaignMemberId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign member to be deleted.

200.description: Indicates the campaign member was successfully deleted.

404.description: Indicates the campaign member was not found in the specified campaign.

post.summary: Forwards a response to a specified email address.
post.description:
This endpoint allows forwarding a specific campaign member's response to an external email address. It retrieves
the response
based on the campaign member ID, constructs an email with the provided message, and schedules it for sending. The
sender is identified
by the name associated with the current user's identity. This action is auditable and can be tracked for compliance
purposes.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

parameters.name: campaignMemberId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign member whose response is being forwarded.

requestBody.description: The model containing the email address to forward to and the message to include.

204.description: Indicates the response was successfully forwarded.

404.description: Indicates the specified response could not be found.

post.summary: Adds respondents to a campaign from a spreadsheet.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the addition of respondents to a specified campaign by uploading a spreadsheet. The
spreadsheet is processed to extract
respondent information which is then queued for import into the campaign. This method does not support P-GAP
validation for the uploaded respondents.
The operation logs the upload activity and schedules the respondents for addition to the campaign. It requires the
spreadsheet file to be attached
in the request body with type 'form-data'.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign to which respondents are being added.

parameters.name: sendEmailAt
parameters.description: Optional parameter to specify when the emails to the newly added respondents should be sent.

200.description: Indicates that the respondents were successfully added or the request contained no files.

400.description: Returned when no spreadsheet file is found in the request.

500.description: Returned when an unexpected error occurs during the addition process.

post.summary: Retrieves a sample of respondents from a spreadsheet uploaded for a campaign.
post.description:
This endpoint processes a spreadsheet file uploaded for a specific campaign to extract a sample of respondent data.
It validates the presence of required headers
based on the campaign's configuration and checks the validity of respondent information, such as phone numbers or
email addresses, depending on the campaign type.
The method returns a sample of valid rows, a count of invalid rows, and the total number of valid respondents
identified. This endpoint does not support P-GAP validation.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign for which the spreadsheet is uploaded.

200.description: Returns the sample data along with counts of valid and invalid rows.

400.description: Returned when no spreadsheet file is found in the request.

500.description: Returned when an unexpected error occurs during sample extraction.

post.summary: Generates a report based on the specified report name and campaign IDs.
post.description:
This endpoint supports generating various types of reports for given campaign IDs within a specified date range.
The type of report generated depends on the
report name provided. Reports can range from response summaries to detailed analytics across different dimensions
such as employee performance, industry,
segment, and campaign-specific metrics. The method dynamically selects the appropriate report generation strategy
based on the report name and prepares
an Excel file as output.

parameters.name: reportName
parameters.description: The name of the report to be generated.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The start date for the report data range. Optional.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The end date for the report data range. Optional.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs for which the report is to be generated.

200.description: Returns the generated report file.

500.description: Returned if there is an error during report generation.

post.summary: Schedules reminders for a campaign within a specified time range.
post.description:
This endpoint schedules reminders for all respondents of a campaign who have not yet completed their survey.
Reminders are scheduled to be sent out
at a specific time within the provided start and end time range. The operation ensures that reminders are only sent
during the specified window and
adheres to any configured reminder policies for the campaign.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign for which reminders are being scheduled.

requestBody.description: The schedule details including start time, end time, and send time for the reminders.

200.description: Indicates that the reminders were successfully scheduled.

400.description: Returned if there is an issue with the provided data, such as invalid time range.

post.summary: Provides a preview of the reminders that would be sent out for a campaign within a specified time range.
post.description:
This endpoint generates a preview of the reminder messages that would be sent out to respondents of a campaign
based on the provided schedule.
The preview includes details such as the number of reminders, the intended recipients, and the content of the
reminder messages. This operation
does not schedule or send any reminders but is intended for review and validation purposes before actual reminders
are scheduled.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the campaign for which the reminder preview is generated.

requestBody.description: The schedule details including start time, end time for the reminder preview.

200.description: Returns the preview of the reminders that would be sent out.

400.description: Returned if there is an issue with the provided data, such as invalid time range.

put.summary:
Edits an existing campaign. One difference in the v2 endpoint is that it also allows you to delete categories from
the related template.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign to edit.

requestBody.description: The updated campaign model.

200.description: Returns the updated campaign.

400.description: If the model is invalid.

404.description: If the campaign is not found.

put.description:
Edits an existing campaign. One difference in the v2 endpoint is that it also allows you to delete categories from
the related template.

parameters.name: key

202.description: Indicates that the update was accepted and processed successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves details of a specific company by its ID.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches detailed information about a company, identified by its unique ID, that belongs to the
organization of the currently authenticated user.
It includes information such as company name, managers, and other relevant details. If the company is not found
within the organization, a 404 Not Found status is returned.

parameters.name: companyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the company.

200.description: Returns the company details.

404.description: Returned if the company is not found.

put.summary: Edits the details of an existing company.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the modification of an existing company's details. It requires the company ID and the new
details to be provided.
The operation will update the company information within the organization of the currently authenticated user. This
action is auditable.

parameters.name: companyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the company to be edited.

requestBody.description: The new details for the company.

200.description: Indicates the company details were successfully updated.

delete.summary: Deletes a company by its id.

parameters.name: companyId
parameters.description: The id of the company

200.description: OK

delete.description: Deletes a company by its id.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all companies within the organization of the currently authenticated user.
get.description:
This endpoint returns a list of all companies associated with the organization of the currently authenticated user.
It includes details such as company names,
attached employees, and other relevant information. If no companies are found within the organization, a 404 Not
Found status is returned.

200.description: Returns the list of companies.

404.description: Returned if no companies are found.

post.summary: Creates a new company with the provided details.
post.description:
This endpoint is responsible for creating a new company within the organization of the currently authenticated
user. It requires the company details to be provided in the request body.
If a company with the same name already exists within the organization, a conflict response is returned to prevent
duplicate entries. Upon successful creation, the new company's details are returned.

requestBody.description: The details of the company to be created.

200.description: Returns the details of the newly created company.

409.description: Returned if a company with the same name already exists.

get.summary: Retrieves all responses associated with a specific company.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a list of all responses submitted for the campaigns run by a specific company. It is useful
for analyzing the overall
feedback and performance metrics of the company's campaigns. The company is identified by its unique ID.

parameters.name: companyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the company.

200.description: Returns the list of responses.

get.summary: Retrieves all respondents associated with a specific company.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a list of all respondents who have been targeted in the campaigns run by a specific company.
It provides insights into the
demographics and reach of the company's campaigns. The company is identified by its unique ID.

parameters.name: companyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the company.

200.description: Returns the list of respondents.

get.summary: Retrieves the dashboard settings by its unique identifier.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches the configuration settings of a specific dashboard identified by its unique ID. It is
designed to be accessible
to all authenticated users, allowing them to retrieve configuration details for dashboards they have access to. The
method asynchronously
returns the dashboard settings encapsulated within a `DashboardSettingModel`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the dashboard to retrieve.

200.description: Returns the dashboard settings if the specified ID was found.

404.description: Returned if no dashboard settings with the specified ID were found.

delete.summary: Deletes a dashboard by its unique identifier.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows for the deletion of a dashboard identified by its unique ID. It is accessible only to users
with the CompanyAdmin role,
ensuring that only authorized personnel can delete dashboards. The operation is asynchronous and utilizes a
cancellationToken
to support request cancellation. Upon successful deletion, a 204 No Content status is returned to indicate that the
operation was successful
but there is no content to return.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the dashboard to delete.

200.description: OK

put.summary: Edits the settings of an existing dashboard.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the modification of an existing dashboard's settings using the provided JSON document. It
is accessible only to users with the CompanyAdmin role,
ensuring that only authorized personnel can edit dashboards. The operation is asynchronous and utilizes a
cancellationToken
to support request cancellation. Upon successful modification, the updated dashboard settings are returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the dashboard to edit.

requestBody.description: The JSON document containing the updated settings for the dashboard.

200.description: OK

post.summary: Creates a new dashboard with the specified settings.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the creation of a new dashboard using the provided JSON settings. It is accessible only to
users with the CompanyAdmin role,
ensuring that only authorized personnel can create dashboards. The operation is asynchronous and utilizes a
cancellationToken
to support request cancellation. Upon successful creation, the newly created dashboard's settings are returned as a
`DashboardSettingModel`.

requestBody.description: The JSON document containing the settings for the new dashboard.

200.description: OK

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all dashboards.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a list of all dashboard settings available in the system. It is designed to be accessible to
all authenticated users,
allowing them to retrieve a comprehensive list of dashboards. The method asynchronously returns a collection of
dashboard settings,
each encapsulated within a `DashboardSettingModel`.

200.description: Returns a list of all dashboard settings.

post.summary: Retrieves a paginated summary of responses based on the provided filters.
post.description:
This endpoint fetches a paginated summary of responses for specified campaigns, applying filters such as date
range, text search, and category filters.
It supports pagination through 'skip' and 'take' parameters, allowing for efficient data retrieval in scenarios
requiring large datasets.
The operation is asynchronous and leverages a cancellationToken to support request
cancellation.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The start date filter for responses.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The end date filter for responses.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The text search filter for responses.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The ordering of responses, by rating time or score.

parameters.name: uncategorizedOnly
parameters.description: Filter for only uncategorized responses.

parameters.name: catOnly
parameters.description: Filter for only categorized responses.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs to filter responses by.

200.description: Returns the paginated summary of responses.

post.summary: Fetches an analytics summary for specified campaigns within a given date range.
post.description:
This endpoint provides an analytics summary for a list of campaign IDs, optionally filtered by a start and end
date. It is designed to support
data-driven decision-making by providing key metrics and insights into campaign performance. The operation is
asynchronous and leverages a
cancellationToken to support request cancellation. The summary includes metrics such as Net
Promoter Score (NPS) and response rates.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The start date for the date range filter.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end date for the date range filter.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs for which to fetch the analytics summary.

200.description: Returns the analytics summary for the specified campaigns.

post.summary: Fetches responses for specified campaigns based on dashboard configuration.
post.description:
This endpoint retrieves a paginated list of responses for the specified campaigns, according to the configuration
of a given dashboard. It allows
for filtering responses by date range and text search, and supports pagination through 'page' and 'take'
parameters. The operation is asynchronous
and leverages a cancellationToken to support request cancellation. This endpoint is designed to
provide detailed insights into
campaign responses, facilitating data-driven analysis and decision-making.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the paginated results to retrieve.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page.

parameters.name: dashboardId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the dashboard configuration to apply.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The start date filter for responses.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The end date filter for responses.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The text search filter for responses.

requestBody.description: A list of campaign IDs to filter responses by.

200.description:
Returns the paginated list of responses based on the specified filters and dashboard
configuration.

404.description: Returned if the specified dashboard configuration is not found.

post.summary: Creates a token for an employee to use in the web client.
post.description:
This method generates a token for an employee with a configurable expiration time. It reads the request body if the
content type is JSON
to extract the employee email and includes it as a claim in the token. The method returns an
`EmployeeTokenModel` containing the token,
a message indicating the token's expiration, and a clone of the JSON body if present.

200.description: Returns the generated token and related information.

post.summary: Creates a temporary web client token for the specified email.
post.description:
This method sends a temporary web client token to the specified email address. The token can be used for
authentication
in scenarios where temporary access is granted to the web client.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address to send the token to.

200.description: Indicates that the token was successfully sent to the specified email address.

post.summary: Creates a web token for the employee.
post.description:
Generates a web token for the employee based on the provided organization ID (if specified). This token can be used
for authentication within the web client. If the employee does not have access or if the organization ID is not
valid,
a 403 Forbidden status is returned.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Optional organization ID for specifying organization.

200.description: Returns the web token for the employee.

403.description: Indicates that the employee does not have access to the platform.

post.summary: Creates an employee token using API key authentication.
post.description:
Generates a web token for the employee based on the provided organization ID (if specified). This token can be used
for authentication within the feedback app. If the employee does not have access or if the organization ID is not
valid,
a 403 Forbidden status is returned.

parameters.name: employeeEmail
parameters.description: The email of the employee

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Optional organization ID for specifying organization.

200.description: Returns the employee token.

403.description: Indicates that the employee does not have access to the platform.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all employees within the organization.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a list of all employees associated with the organization of the currently authenticated user.
It returns a collection of employee models containing detailed information about each employee.

200.description: Returns the list of employees within the organization.

get.summary: Retrieves details of a specific employee by their ID.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches detailed information about a specific employee identified by their unique ID.
It returns an employee model containing the employee's information if found.

parameters.name: employeeId
parameters.description: The ID of the employee to retrieve.

200.description: Returns the details of the specified employee.

put.summary: Edits an employee's details.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the modification of an existing employee's details. It accepts an employee ID and a model
containing the updated employee information.
The operation will update the employee's record in the database with the provided details. Upon successful update,
the modified employee model is returned.

parameters.name: employeeId
parameters.description: The ID of the employee to edit.

requestBody.description: The updated employee data.

200.description: Indicates the employee was successfully updated.

get.summary: Gets analytics for an employee by days.
get.description:
This method provides analytics for a specific employee or the currently authenticated employee based on the number
of days specified.
It can filter analytics by employee ID, email, or external ID. If none of these are provided, it defaults to the
currently authenticated employee's email.
The analytics include ratings from responses starting from the current date going back the specified number of
days.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
Optional. The ID of the employee. If not provided, the method attempts to use the email or external
ID.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description:
Optional. The email of the employee. If not provided, the method attempts to use the employee ID or
external ID.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description:
Optional. The external ID of the employee. If not provided, the method attempts to use the
employee ID or email.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: The number of days to look back for analytics. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

200.description: Returns the analytics data for the specified employee or the currently authenticated employee.

400.description: Returned if the required parameters are not provided or are invalid.

put.summary: Merges the source Employee into target Employee.
put.description:
This method merges the data of a source employee into a target employee. It is useful for consolidating duplicate
records or transferring data between employee records.
After the merge, the source employee's data will be integrated into the target employee's record.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: The ID of the source employee.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: The ID of the target employee.

200.description: Returns the merged employee.

500.description: Returned if the merge operation fails due to invalid input or other errors.

get.summary: Checks if an employee is in a P-GAP period for a specific campaign.
get.description:
This method determines whether an employee is currently within a P-GAP (Performance Gap Analysis Period) for a
given campaign.
A P-GAP period is a designated time frame during which an employee's performance metrics are analyzed for
significant deviations.
The method requires identification of the employee, which can be provided through an ID, email, or external ID.
Additionally, the campaign ID must be specified.
If the necessary parameters are not provided or if the campaign does not exist or is inaccessible to the requester,
the method returns an error.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the employee.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email of the employee.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: The external ID of the employee.

200.description: Indicates that the check was successful, returning whether the employee is in a P-GAP period.

400.description:
Returned if the required parameters are not provided or are invalid, or if the campaign does not
exist or is inaccessible.

get.summary: Retrieves P-GAP periods for employees within a specified date range and campaign.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches P-GAP (Performance Gap Analysis Period) periods for employees, optionally filtered by
campaign and whether the period is active.
It supports pagination through 'skip' and 'take' parameters, allowing for efficient data retrieval in scenarios
with large datasets.
The method validates the 'take' parameter to ensure it does not exceed the maximum allowed value to prevent
performance issues.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: The start date of the period to filter by.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: The end date of the period to filter by.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: Optional. The ID of the campaign to filter the P-GAP periods by.

parameters.name: employeeId
parameters.description: Optional. The ID of the employee to filter the P-GAP periods by.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Optional. Specifies whether to filter by active P-GAP periods.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of records to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of records to return. Limited to a maximum of 1000 to ensure performance.

200.description: Returns the filtered list of P-GAP periods.

400.description: Returned if the 'take' parameter exceeds the maximum allowed value or other validation fails.

post.summary: Creates P-GAP periods for employees.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the creation of P-GAP (Performance Gap Analysis Period) periods for employees. P-GAP
periods are used to analyze and identify any performance gaps within a specified timeframe.
The method accepts a list of P-GAP period creation models and attempts to create these periods for the organization
associated with the currently authenticated user.
If any of the P-GAP periods are successfully created, the method returns a 200 OK status with the details of the
created periods.
If none of the periods can be created due to validation errors or other issues, a 400 Bad Request status is
returned.

requestBody.description: The list of P-GAP periods to create.

200.description: Indicates that one or more P-GAP periods were successfully created.

400.description:
Indicates that none of the P-GAP periods could be created due to validation errors or other
issues.

delete.summary: Deletes the specified P-GAP periods by their IDs.
delete.description:
This method allows for the deletion of specified P-GAP (Performance Gap Analysis Period) periods by their unique
identifiers.
It is intended for use in scenarios where certain P-GAP periods are no longer relevant or were created in error.
The operation will check for the existence of the specified P-GAP period IDs within the organization of the
currently authenticated user.
If any of the specified IDs do not exist, a NotFound response is returned. On successful deletion, a confirmation
message is returned.

requestBody.description: The IDs of the P-GAP periods to delete.

200.description: Indicates that the P-GAP periods were successfully deleted.

404.description:
Indicates that none of the requested P-GAP period IDs matched any existing periods in the
organization.

400.description: Indicates that the deletion operation failed, with details provided in the error message.

put.summary: Updates the P-GAP periods for employees.
put.description:
This method updates the Performance Gap Analysis Period (P-GAP) for a list of employees. P-GAP periods are used to
assess and address performance gaps within a specified timeframe.
The method validates each P-GAP period to ensure that the start date is before the end date. If any period fails
this validation, a BadRequest is returned with details.
On successful update, the method returns the updated list of P-GAP periods. This operation requires the caller to
be authorized and to have access to the specified organization's data.

requestBody.description: The list of P-GAP periods to update.

200.description: Indicates that the P-GAP periods were successfully updated.

400.description: Indicates that the update operation failed due to invalid input or other errors.

get.summary: Retrieves the responses by account number.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches responses associated with a given account, identified by either an email or an external ID.
It allows filtering responses to include only empty ones or all responses. Pagination is supported through 'skip'
and 'take' parameters.
This method is useful for analyzing respondent feedback or activity by their account information.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address of the respondent. Optional if 'externalId' is provided.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: The external ID of the respondent. Optional if 'email' is provided.

parameters.name: empty
parameters.description: A flag indicating whether to retrieve empty responses or not.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of responses to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of responses to take. Used for pagination.

200.description: Returns the list of responses associated with the specified account number.

400.description:
Returned if both 'email' and 'externalId' parameters are missing, or other validation errors
occur.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all available languages.
get.description:
This endpoint provides a comprehensive list of languages supported or recognized by the system. It is useful for
applications that need to display
language options to the user, such as for setting a preferred language. The method returns a list of language
entities, each containing language
details such as name and language code.

200.description: Returns the list of available languages.

404.description: Returned if no languages are found.

get.summary: Retrieves details of a specific language by its ID.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches details of a language identified by its unique ID. It is useful for obtaining specific
information about a language,
such as its name, code, and any other relevant details stored in the system. This can be particularly useful for
editing language details
or for displaying language-specific content.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the language.

200.description: Returns the details of the specified language.

404.description: Returned if the language with the specified ID is not found.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of logo URIs for the current organization.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches and returns a list of URIs for logos associated with the current organization. It is useful
for
displaying all logos that an organization has uploaded. The method is accessible to authenticated users and
leverages the organization ID from the
user's identity to query the database.

200.description: Returns the list of logo URIs associated with the current organization.

post.summary: Uploads a new logo image for the current organization.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the uploading of a new logo image file for the organization. The uploaded file is
processed and stored, with its URI being
saved for future reference. This operation is essential for organizations looking to update their branding or add
new logo variations.

202.description: Indicates the logo upload process was successfully initiated.

delete.summary: Deletes a specified logo from the current organization.
delete.description:
This endpoint deletes a logo identified by its file name from the organization's collection of logos. It first
checks if the logo exists and whether
it is currently in use by any campaigns. If the logo is in use, the operation is aborted to prevent breaking
campaign designs. Otherwise, the logo is
deleted, and confirmation is returned.

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: The file name of the logo to be deleted.

200.description: Indicates the logo was successfully deleted.

404.description: Indicates the specified logo could not be found.

409.description: Indicates the logo is currently in use by one or more campaigns and cannot be deleted.

400.description: Indicates the logo could not be deleted due to an error, such as an incorrect file name.

delete.summary: Revokes access for a specified user by deleting their account.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows for the removal of a user's access to the organization by deleting their account.
It requires the caller to have the CompanyAdmin role. Upon successful deletion, it returns a confirmation message.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The ID of the user to delete.

200.description: Returns a confirmation message indicating the user was deleted.

get.summary: Retrieves the profile of the current organization.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches the profile details of the organization based on the organization ID obtained from the
current context.
It is useful for displaying organization-specific information on the dashboard or profile settings page.

200.description: Returns the organization's profile details.

404.description: If the organization's profile could not be found.

put.summary: Edits the profile of the organization.
put.description:
This method is deprecated and should not be used in new development. Use M:npstoday_v2.API.Controllers.OrganizationController.EditOrganization(npstoday_v2.API.Core.Configuration.DomainModels.Organizations.OrganizationModel,System.Threading.CancellationToken) instead
for editing organization details.
The operation is asynchronous and supports cancellation.

requestBody.description: The model containing the updated organization profile information.

200.description: Indicates that the organization's profile was successfully updated.

put.summary: Edits the call duration limit for the organization.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the modification of the organization's call duration limit, which is used when sending
surveys using a phone system. The limit is specified in seconds.
A positive integer is required to set the new limit. If a negative value is provided, a BadRequest is returned.

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: The new call duration limit in seconds.

200.description: Returns a confirmation message with the new limit.

400.description: Returned if the provided limit is invalid (e.g., negative).

get.summary: Retrieves the current call duration limit for the organization.
get.description:
This method fetches the organization's configured call duration limit, which is used when sending surveys using a
phone system. The limit is returned in seconds and represents the maximum allowed duration for calls within the
organization. This setting is crucial for managing call costs and ensuring efficient use of communication
resources. The method returns the current setting, allowing clients to adjust their call handling procedures
accordingly.

200.description: Returns the current call duration limit in seconds.

put.summary: Edits the organization's data anonymization settings.
put.description:
This method allows for the configuration of the organization's data anonymization policy by specifying the number
of days after which the data should be anonymized.
It supports setting a specific duration in days or removing the limit entirely for disabling anonymization.
This is crucial for compliance with data protection regulations and ensuring that the organization's data handling
practices align with legal and ethical standards.

requestBody.description: The model containing the number of days after which data should be anonymized.

200.description: Returns a success message with the new anonymization settings.

400.description: Returned if the provided days parameter is invalid (e.g., negative).

get.summary: Retrieves the organization's data anonymization settings.
get.description:
This endpoint provides the current configuration for data anonymization within the organization, specifically the
number of days after which data should be anonymized. This setting is crucial for compliance with privacy laws and
regulations, ensuring that personal data is not retained indefinitely. The method returns the number of days set
for anonymization, allowing for review and adjustment as needed to meet organizational and regulatory requirements.

200.description: Returns the current anonymization setting in days.

get.summary: Retrieves the current seat allocation for the organization.
get.description:
This endpoint provides the total number of seats (licenses) allocated to the organization, allowing for management
and planning of resource allocation. It is essential for administrators to monitor seat usage to ensure compliance
with licensing agreements and to manage costs effectively. The method returns the current seat count, which
includes both used and unused seats.

200.description: Returns the total number of seats allocated to the organization.

delete.summary: Deletes an employee from the organization.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows for the removal of an employee's record from the organization. It is intended for use by
administrators
to manage their organization's workforce. The deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. Upon successful deletion,
a confirmation
is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the employee to delete.

200.description: Indicates the employee was successfully deleted.

put.summary: Edits an existing employee's details.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the modification of an existing employee's details based on the provided employee model.
It requires the ID of the employee to be edited and the new details to be applied.
The operation is asynchronous and leverages a cancellationToken to support request
cancellation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the employee to be edited.

requestBody.description: The model containing the new details for the employee.

200.description: Indicates that the employee's details were successfully updated.

400.description: Indicates that the request was malformed or the model validation failed.

404.description: Indicates that the employee with the specified ID was not found.

post.summary: Adds a new employee to the organization.
post.description:
This endpoint is used to add a new employee to the organization. It accepts an employee model containing the
necessary information
to create a new employee record. Upon successful creation, it logs the activity and returns the newly created
employee's details.
This is essential for maintaining an up-to-date record of all employees within the organization.

requestBody.description: The employee model containing the details of the new employee.

200.description: Returns the newly created employee's details.

get.summary: Retrieves a user associated with the current organization by their ID.
get.description:
This endpoint is used to fetch the details of a specific user by their unique identifier, provided they are
associated with the organization
that the current user is logged into. It is useful for administrative purposes such as user management and
auditing. If the user is not found,
a 404 Not Found status is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to retrieve.

200.description: Returns the user's details associated with the given ID.

404.description: Returned if no user with the given ID is found within the organization.

get.summary: Retrieves all users associated with the currently logged-in organization.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a list of users who have access to the organization that the current user is logged into. It
is useful for administrators
or managers who need to view or manage the list of users within their organization.

200.description: Returns the list of users associated with the organization.

get.summary: Retrieves all users who are direct children of the currently logged-in organization.
get.description:
This endpoint is designed to fetch a list of users who are considered direct children of the organization
associated with the current user's session.
It is particularly useful for hierarchical organizations looking to manage or review the users within their
immediate organizational structure.
The method ensures that only users with a direct relationship to the organization are returned, facilitating
focused administrative tasks.

200.description: Returns the list of direct child users of the organization.

put.summary: Edits the organization with the provided model.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the updating of the organization's details based on the provided organization model. It is
intended for use by administrators
with the necessary permissions to modify organizational settings. The method fetches the current organization,
applies the updates, and persists
the changes to the database. A successful update returns the updated organization model.

requestBody.description: The model containing the updated organization details.

200.description: Returns the updated organization model.

get.summary: Retrieves the organization details for the currently logged-in user.
get.description:
This method fetches the organization details associated with the currently logged-in user. It is useful for
obtaining organization-specific
information such as name, address, and other relevant details. If the organization cannot be found, a 404 Not Found
status is returned, indicating
that the user may not be associated with any organization or there is an issue with the organization's data.

200.description: Returns the organization details associated with the currently logged-in user.

404.description: Returned if the organization cannot be found.

get.summary: Retrieves the current provider information for the specified provider type.
get.description:
This endpoint is used to fetch the current SMS or Email provider configured for the organization. It accepts a
provider type as a path variable
and returns the corresponding provider's details, including the organization ID, provider type, and provider name.
This is useful for organizations
looking to verify or review their current communication service setups. If an invalid provider type is specified,
an exception is thrown.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: The type of provider to retrieve information for, e.g., "smsprovider" or "emailprovider".

200.description: Returns the current provider information for the specified type.

400.description: Returned if an invalid provider type is specified.

post.summary: Processes and queues a spreadsheet for import.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the uploading of a spreadsheet file to be processed and queued for import into the
organization's data. The spreadsheet
must contain at least one of the following columns: 'employee_email', 'respondent_email', 'company_name'. The
method checks for the presence of these
columns to ensure the spreadsheet contains relevant data.

200.description: Indicates the spreadsheet was successfully queued for import.

400.description: Returned if the spreadsheet does not contain the required columns.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of authorized senders for the organization.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches and returns a list of authorized senders associated with the organization of the currently
logged-in user. Authorized senders
are entities that have been granted permission to send communications on behalf of the organization. This
information is crucial for managing and
overseeing the organization's communication strategies. The method queries the sender repository for all authorized
senders linked to the organization
and returns them in a list format.

200.description: Returns the list of authorized senders for the organization.

post.summary: Adds a list of authorized senders for the organization.
post.description:
This endpoint allows a super administrator to add a list of authorized senders to the organization. Authorized
senders are entities or individuals
permitted to send communications on behalf of the organization. This method takes a list of
npstoday_v2.API.Core.Configuration.DomainModels.AuthorizedSenderModel objects, processes them,
and updates the organization's list of authorized senders accordingly. The operation returns the updated list of
authorized senders for the organization.

requestBody.description: A list of senders to be authorized.

200.description: Indicates the senders were successfully added and returns the updated list.

put.summary: Edits an existing authorized sender for the organization.
put.description:
This endpoint allows a super administrator to update the details of an existing authorized sender within the
organization. Authorized senders are entities
or individuals that have been granted permission to send communications on behalf of the organization. The method
updates the sender's information based on
the provided model and sender ID. It is essential for maintaining accurate and up-to-date information on who can
represent the organization in communications.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the sender to be edited.

requestBody.description: The updated sender information.

200.description: Indicates the sender was successfully updated.

404.description: Indicates the specified sender was not found.

delete.summary: Deletes an authorized sender from the organization.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows a super administrator to remove an authorized sender from the organization's list. Authorized
senders are entities or individuals
that have been granted permission to send communications on behalf of the organization. Removing a sender is a
critical action that should be taken
with caution, as it immediately revokes the sender's authorization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the sender to be deleted.

200.description: Indicates the sender was successfully deleted.

404.description: Indicates the specified sender was not found.

get.summary: Retrieves usage statistics for the organization within a specified date range.
get.description:
This endpoint provides detailed usage statistics for the organization, including data on various metrics such as
number of users, activity levels,
and resource consumption. The statistics can be filtered by specifying a date range, allowing organizations to
analyze trends and patterns over time.
This information is crucial for understanding the organization's operational dynamics and for making informed
decisions regarding resource allocation
and management. If no date range is specified, the method returns the total usage statistics available.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
The end date of the period for which usage statistics are requested. If null, defaults to the
current date.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
The start date of the period for which usage statistics are requested. If null, includes all data
up to the 'before' date.

200.description: Returns the usage statistics for the organization.

post.summary: Tests the relay functionality with a given campaign member.
post.description:
This endpoint is used for testing the relay service by sending a campaign member's information. It accepts a
`CampaignMemberModel`
object as input, which is then mapped to a `CampaignMember` entity. The service attempts to relay the
participant's information
to the specified destination. This method is primarily used for debugging and testing purposes to ensure that the
relay service is functioning
correctly. A successful relay returns the result of the operation.

requestBody.description: The campaign member's information to be relayed.

200.description: Indicates the relay operation was successful.

400.description: Indicates the payload could not be parsed to a proper participant.

post.summary: Validates the format of an uploaded spreadsheet for employee data.
post.description:
This endpoint is used to validate the format of a spreadsheet uploaded for the purpose of adding or updating
employee data. It checks for the presence
of required columns such as 'respondent_email' or 'respondent_phone'. The method processes the uploaded file to
ensure it meets the expected format
and contains the necessary information for further processing. A sample of the spreadsheet data (up to 5 rows) is
returned along with the validation result.
This is useful for providing immediate feedback on the upload format before processing the entire file.

200.description:
Returns a sample of the spreadsheet data and the total number of rows, indicating the file format
is valid.

400.description: Returned if the spreadsheet is missing required columns.
