get.summary: Retrieves the profile information of the current organization.
get.description:
This endpoint is responsible for fetching the profile details of the organization associated with the currently
authenticated user. It includes
information such as the organization's name, contact details, and other relevant settings. This is crucial for
allowing organizations to view their
current configuration and details within the system. If the organization's profile cannot be found, a 404 Not Found
status is returned.

200.description: Returns the organization's profile information.

404.description: Returned if the organization's profile could not be found.

put.summary: Edits the profile of the current organization.
put.description:
This endpoint allows for the modification of the current organization's profile information. It accepts an
`OrganizationModel` object
containing the updated profile details. The method updates the organization's profile in the database and returns
the updated profile information.
This operation is essential for organizations to keep their profile information current and accurate.

requestBody.description: The model containing the updated organization profile information.

200.description: Returns the updated organization profile information.

post.summary: Uploads and updates the organization's logo.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the uploading of a new logo image for the organization. The uploaded image replaces any
existing logo. It is important
for maintaining the organization's branding and identity within the system. The method accepts a file through a
form submission and processes it
to update the organization's logo. The operation is asynchronous and returns an Accepted status upon successful
initiation of the upload process.

202.description: Indicates the logo upload process was successfully initiated.

post.summary: Associates a user from another organization with the current organization.
post.description:
This endpoint is used to create an association between the current organization and a user from another
organization. It requires the credentials
of the user to be associated, and optionally, a name for the association. This is useful for organizations looking
to collaborate or share resources
with users from other organizations. The association process involves validating the provided credentials and, upon
success, creating a link between
the organizations. If the credentials are incorrect or the association fails, an appropriate error message is
returned.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: An optional name for the association.

requestBody.description: The credentials of the user to be associated.

200.description: Indicates the association was successfully created.

400.description: Indicates incorrect credentials were provided or the association could not be created.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all associations related to the current organization.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches and returns all associations linked to the current organization. Associations are other
organizations that have been linked
through various means, such as partnerships or collaborations. This information is vital for understanding the
network and connections of the
organization within the broader ecosystem. The method returns a list of associations, including their names, IDs,
and other relevant details.

200.description: Returns the list of associations related to the current organization.

delete.summary: Unassociates an organization from the current organization.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows a company administrator to remove an existing association with another organization.
Associations are typically used for collaboration
or sharing resources between organizations. Removing an association may affect access to shared resources or
collaborative features. This action should be
considered carefully. The operation is irreversible through this API once completed.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization to unassociate.

200.description: Indicates the organization was successfully unassociated.

put.summary: Edits the label of an existing association with another organization.
put.description:
This endpoint allows a company administrator to change the label of an existing association with another
organization. The label is a user-friendly name
or identifier for the association, making it easier to recognize and manage. This operation is useful for
maintaining clear and meaningful names for
associations, especially when the number of associations grows. It requires the ID of the organization to be edited
and the new label name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The new label for the association.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization whose association label is to be edited.

200.description: Indicates the association label was successfully updated.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of campaigns accessible to the current organization.
get.description:
This endpoint provides a list of all campaigns that the current organization has access to. It is useful for
organizations to understand and manage
their campaign engagements. The list includes minimal details about each campaign, such as campaign ID and name, to
facilitate quick identification
and selection of campaigns for further actions or analysis. This operation is read-only and does not modify any
data.

200.description: Returns the list of accessible campaigns.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of users associated with the current organization.
get.description:
This endpoint is accessible only to users with the CompanyAdmin role and returns a list of users within the same
organization. Each user's information includes
their user ID, email, creation date, role type, status, and a flag indicating whether the user's profile can be
edited. The list is ordered by the creation date
of the users. This method is used by the old dashboard project and may be subject to future deprecation or changes.

200.description: Returns the list of users associated with the current organization.

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of campaign responses based on various filters.
get.description:
This endpoint provides a flexible retrieval of campaign responses, supporting filtering by date, response status
(empty or not), categorization, and more. It allows for detailed analysis and reporting on campaign data,
particularly useful for integration with Power BI or similar tools. The method supports advanced query
capabilities, including searching by text and filtering by external identifiers, which are crucial for integrating
with external CRM systems like SuperOffice. The response data is paginated to manage large datasets effectively.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of items to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Filter responses after this date.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Filter responses before this date.

parameters.name: empty
parameters.description: If true, includes only responses that are empty.

parameters.name: uncategorizedOnly
parameters.description: If true, includes only uncategorized responses.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The order by which to sort the responses.

parameters.name: includeDoNotContact
parameters.description: If true, includes responses marked as 'Do Not Contact'.

parameters.name: searchString
parameters.description: A search string to filter responses by.

parameters.name: modifiedAfter
parameters.description: Filter responses modified after this date.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: An external identifier to filter responses by.

parameters.name: historic
parameters.description: If true, includes responses based on a historical context.

200.description: Returns the paginated list of campaign responses.

parameters.description: if true then unsubscribe

404.description: Not Found

get.summary: Redirects requests from legacy ULR links to the new survey page.
get.description:
This method handles redirection for customers who have legacy ULR links pointing to old survey pages.
It constructs a new URL using the provided campaign identifier and redirects the user to the updated survey page.
This ensures compatibility and a seamless transition for users with existing links.

parameters.name: campaign
parameters.description: The campaign identifier used to construct the new survey URL.

302.description: Redirects to the new survey page.

get.summary: Redirects requests from legacy reply page links to the new survey reply page.
get.description:
This method handles redirection for customers who have links pointing to old survey reply pages, such as those
generated by "GeneratePage.aspx".
It constructs a new URL by appending the original query string to the configured survey URL and redirects the user
to the updated reply page.
This ensures that users with existing links are seamlessly redirected to the correct location without losing any
query parameters.

302.description: Redirects to the new survey reply page.

post.summary: Creates a new relay setting.
post.description:
This method creates a new relay setting based on the provided relay setting model. It first checks if a current
relay setting already exists to prevent duplicates. If a current setting exists, it returns a conflict response.
Otherwise, it proceeds to create a new relay setting using the provided model. The method maps the model to the
appropriate relay setting type, creates the setting asynchronously, and returns the created setting mapped back to
a model. This endpoint is essential for configuring new relay settings that determine how messages are relayed
within the system.

requestBody.description: The relay setting model to create a new setting from.

200.description: OK

get.summary: Retrieves the current relay setting.
get.description:
This method fetches the current relay setting from the system. It is designed to provide access to the
configuration that determines how messages are relayed within the system. This functionality is crucial for system
administrators or users with the appropriate permissions to view and verify the current relay settings, ensuring
that the message relay operations are configured as intended. The method utilizes a cancellation token to support
task cancellation if necessary.

200.description: OK

put.summary: Updates an existing relay setting.
put.description:
This method updates an existing relay setting with the details provided in the
relaySettingModel. It first maps the provided model to the appropriate relay setting type based
on the relay type specified in the model. Then, it calls the relay setting service to apply the updates
asynchronously. This operation is crucial for maintaining the accuracy and relevance of relay settings as system
requirements evolve. The method ensures that the updated relay setting is persisted and reflects the changes
requested. Upon successful update, the updated relay setting is returned, mapped back to a model for client
consumption.

requestBody.description: The model containing the updated details for the relay setting.

200.description: OK

delete.summary: Deletes the current relay setting.
delete.description:
This method attempts to delete the current relay setting from the system. It is designed for scenarios where the
existing relay configuration needs to be removed, possibly for reconfiguration or discontinuation of the relay
service. The method checks if a relay setting exists and, if found, proceeds with the deletion. If no relay setting
is found, it returns a NotFound response, indicating that there was no setting to delete. This operation is
critical for maintaining the flexibility and accuracy of relay configurations within the system.

200.description: OK

404.description: Not Found

get.summary: Retrieves the default report for specified campaigns within a date range.
get.description:
This method generates a report based on the provided campaign IDs and date range. It supports filtering by start
and end dates
and allows for the inclusion of multiple campaigns. The report is returned as an Excel file. This operation
requires authorization.

parameters.name: campaigns
parameters.description: A list of campaign IDs to include in the report.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The start date for the report period. Optional.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end date for the report period. Optional.

200.description: Returns the generated report as an Excel file.

400.description: Returned if the request parameters are invalid.

401.description: Returned if the user is unauthorized.

put.summary: Merges two respondents into a single respondent record.
put.description:
This method combines the data of two respondents, identified by their unique identifiers, into a single respondent
record. The merge operation is designed to consolidate duplicate or related respondent records, preserving relevant
data and associations from both source and target respondents. The source respondent's data is mapped onto the
target respondent, after which the source respondent is removed. This operation is critical for maintaining data
integrity and reducing redundancy within the respondent database. Special care is taken to handle cases where
either the source or target respondent does not exist, returning a NotFound result in such scenarios.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the source respondent to be merged.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the target respondent to retain.

200.description: OK

404.description: Not Found

put.summary: Edits an existing respondent's details.
put.description:
This method allows for the modification of an existing respondent's details based on the provided
editRespondentModel. It includes functionality to update various respondent attributes such as
responsible employee and company affiliation. Special handling is included to prevent editing the default anonymous
respondent as defined in the system's anonymization configuration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the respondent to be edited.

requestBody.description: The model containing the updated respondent details.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

404.description: Not Found

get.summary: Retrieves the details of a specific respondent by their unique identifier.
get.description:
This method attempts to fetch and return the details of a respondent based on the provided unique identifier. It is
designed to facilitate the retrieval of individual respondent information, supporting operations such as viewing or
editing respondent details within the system. The method handles cases where the respondent may not be found or
belong to the current organization, providing appropriate feedback. Additionally, it incorporates error handling to
manage unexpected issues during the process.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the respondent to retrieve.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

put.summary: Edits the active status of a respondent.
put.description:
This method updates the active status of a respondent identified by their unique identifier. It allows for enabling
or disabling a respondent's active status within the organization, effectively toggling their eligibility for
campaign participation. This functionality is crucial for managing respondent engagement and ensuring accurate
targeting for campaigns. The method performs a check to ensure the respondent exists within the organization before
proceeding with the update. If the respondent is not found, a NotFound response is returned. Otherwise, the active
status is updated, and the changes are saved to the database.

parameters.name: respondentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the respondent whose active status is being edited.

parameters.name: activeStatus
parameters.description: The new active status to be applied to the respondent.

200.description: OK

404.description: Not Found

get.summary: Retrieves a list of respondents, optionally filtered by their active status.
get.description:
This method provides a way to retrieve respondents from the organization. It supports filtering by the active
status of the respondents, allowing for more targeted data retrieval. The method also supports pagination through
the 'skip' and 'take' parameters, enabling efficient data handling and retrieval in scenarios with large datasets.
This endpoint is essential for managing and viewing respondent data within the organization, offering flexibility
in data access patterns.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Optional. Filters respondents based on their active status.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of records to skip for pagination. Default is 0.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: Optional. The number of records to return for pagination.

200.description: OK

delete.summary: Deletes a respondent and their associated campaign memberships from the organization.
delete.description:
This method removes a respondent and all their associated campaign memberships from the database. It checks for the
existence of the respondent and ensures that the default anonymous respondent cannot be deleted, preserving
essential system functionality. This operation is irreversible and should be used with caution.

parameters.name: respondentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the respondent to be deleted.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

get.summary: Retrieves a list of responses for a specific respondent.
get.description:
This method fetches all responses associated with a given respondent ID, ensuring that only responses belonging to
the specified respondent are returned.

parameters.name: respondentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the respondent whose responses are being requested.

200.description: Returns a list of responses for the specified respondent.

get.summary: Retrieves responses based on an external identifier with optional filtering for empty responses.
get.description:
This method fetches responses associated with a given external identifier, allowing for optional filtering to
include or exclude empty responses. It supports pagination through 'skip' and 'take' parameters to efficiently
manage large datasets. This endpoint is particularly useful for external systems or integrations that need to
synchronize or analyze respondent data based on external references. The method ensures that only relevant data is
returned, enhancing performance and usability.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: The external identifier used to filter responses.

parameters.name: empty
parameters.description: A boolean flag to include or exclude empty responses. Default is false.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of records to skip for pagination. Default is 0.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of records to return for pagination. Default is 100.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

put.summary: Unsubscribes a respondent from campaign communications.
put.description:
This method allows a respondent to unsubscribe from further campaign communications by using a unique public
identifier. It sets the respondent's active status to false, effectively removing them from future campaign
distributions. This action supports privacy and user choice by enabling respondents to opt-out of communications.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The public unique identifier of the respondent to unsubscribe.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

404.description: Not Found

200.description: OK

get.summary: Retrieves a collection of review names for organizations that allow public reviews.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches names of all organizations where public reviews are enabled. It filters organizations based
on the 'EnablePublicReviews' flag and non-empty 'ReviewName' fields, returning a list of review names. Useful for
displaying available reviews to end-users without requiring authorization.

200.description: OK

get.summary: Retrieves the organization ID based on the review name.
get.description:
This method searches for an organization by its review name and returns the corresponding organization ID. It's
useful for linking review names to their internal organization identifiers. If the review name does not match any
organization, or an error occurs, a BadRequest is returned.

parameters.name: reviewName
parameters.description: The name of the review associated with the organization.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

get.summary: Retrieves campaign IDs associated with a given organization ID.
get.description:
This endpoint is used to fetch all campaign IDs linked to a specific organization, aiding in the aggregation and
analysis of campaign-related data. It's particularly useful for external services or integrations that require a
list of campaigns for data processing or reporting purposes. In case of an error or if the organization ID does not
exist, a BadRequest is returned.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

get.summary: Retrieves the profile information for a specified organization.
get.description:
This endpoint is designed to fetch detailed profile information of an organization based on its unique identifier
(orgId). It's particularly useful for external entities seeking comprehensive data about an organization, including
its review-related metrics and attributes. The method ensures that only valid orgIds are processed, returning a
BadRequest for invalid or missing IDs, and encapsulates error handling for broader exception scenarios.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization.

200.description: OK

400.description: Bad Request

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of review responses for a specified organization.
get.description:
This endpoint provides access to a subset of review responses associated with a given organization, supporting
pagination for efficient data retrieval. It's designed to facilitate the exploration and analysis of feedback at
scale. The method leverages the 'ResponseService' to apply filters, order the results by rating, and paginate the
response based on the provided 'skip' and 'take' parameters. This approach ensures that consumers can access
relevant data in a manageable format, tailored to their specific needs.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization whose review responses are being requested.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: take

This endpoint provides analytics charts based on responses from multiple organizations for a specific shared
dashboard. It is accessible without authentication,
making it suitable for public dashboards. The analytics are calculated per organization, offering insights into
response trends and distributions across different
campaigns within the shared dashboard's configuration. The operation is asynchronous and leverages a
cancellationToken to support request cancellation.

This endpoint provides detailed analytics data based on responses from multiple organizations for a specific shared
dashboard. It is accessible without authentication,
making it suitable for public dashboards. The analytics data include metrics and insights per organization,
facilitating a comparative analysis across different
campaigns within the shared dashboard's configuration. The operation is asynchronous and utilizes a
cancellationToken to support request cancellation.
This endpoint is crucial for stakeholders to understand performance metrics across organizations.

The operation is asynchronous and leverages a cancellationToken to support request

in scenarios requiring large datasets.

This endpoint fetches the associations (e.g., organizations, teams) linked to a specific shared dashboard. It is
designed to provide stakeholders with
insights into which entities are involved with the shared dashboard, enhancing transparency and collaboration. The
operation is asynchronous and
leverages a cancellationToken to support request cancellation. This endpoint is accessible
without authentication, making it suitable
for scenarios where public access to dashboard associations is required.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of available campaigns for a shared dashboard.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches a list of campaigns that are available within the context of a shared dashboard. It is
designed to provide users with
the ability to view which campaigns are included in the shared dashboard configuration. The operation is
asynchronous and leverages a
cancellationToken to support request cancellation. This endpoint is accessible without
authentication, making it suitable
for scenarios where public access to campaign information is required.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the shared dashboard.

200.description: Returns the list of available campaigns for the shared dashboard.

404.description: Returned if the shared dashboard with the specified ID is not found.

get.summary: Retrieves the settings for a shared dashboard.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches the configuration settings of a shared dashboard identified by its unique ID. It allows users
to understand and manage the settings applied to a shared dashboard,
including visibility and access controls. The operation is asynchronous and leverages a
cancellationToken to support request cancellation. This endpoint is accessible
without authentication, making it suitable for scenarios where settings need to be retrieved for public dashboards.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the shared dashboard.

200.description: Returns the settings for the shared dashboard.

404.description: Returned if the shared dashboard with the specified ID is not found.

post.summary: Shares a dashboard, making it accessible to other users or groups.

This endpoint allows a dashboard to be shared with other users or groups by creating a shared dashboard instance.

This endpoint allows for the revocation of access to a previously shared dashboard, effectively unsharing it.
The operation is asynchronous and leverages a cancellationToken to support request
cancellation.

parameters.name: shareId

cancellation.

get.summary: Retrieves all throttling groups, including a "Default" group, to manage campaign execution rates.
get.description:
Throttling groups control the rate at which campaigns are executed, preventing respondents getting multiple
surveys. This method returns a list of these groups, each with unique properties like name and associated campaign
IDs. A "Default" group is also included as a fallback for campaigns not assigned to any specific group.

200.description: Returns a list of throttling groups.

post.summary: Creates a new throttling group.
post.description:
Allows administrators to define new throttling groups with specific properties, such as name and associated
campaign IDs. This customization facilitates tailored campaign execution according to organizational needs.

requestBody.description:
An `EditOrCreateThrottlingGroupModel` object containing the properties for the new throttling
group. This includes the name of the group, the IDs of campaigns it should manage, and any other relevant settings.

200.description: Returns the created throttling group.

get.summary: Retrieves detailed information about a specific throttling group by ID.
get.description:
This method facilitates the management and review of a single throttling group's settings, providing insights into
its configuration details such as associated campaigns and execution rates.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the throttling group to be retrieved. This ID must correspond to an
existing throttling group within the system.

200.description: Returns the throttling group.

put.summary: Updates the configuration of an existing throttling group.
put.description:
Allows for the modification of properties such as name and associated campaign IDs. This adjustment affects the
execution rate and scope of campaigns managed by the throttling group.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the throttling group to be updated. This ID must correspond to an existing
throttling group within the system.

requestBody.description:
An `EditOrCreateThrottlingGroupModel` object containing the updated properties for the
throttling group. This model includes the new name, campaign IDs, and other settings that should be applied to the
group.

200.description: Returns the updated throttling group.

404.description: Not Found

delete.summary: Removes an existing throttling group from the system.
delete.description:
Throttling groups control the rate at which campaigns are executed, preventing respondents getting multiple
surveys. Removing a group may be necessary when it is no longer needed or if its campaigns have been reassigned.
This operation ensures that the specified throttling group is permanently deleted, along with any associations to
campaigns.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the throttling group to be removed. This ID must correspond to an existing
throttling group within the system.

200.description: Indicates the throttling group was successfully removed.

404.description: Not Found

get.description:
This method is essential for fetching user profile details after successful authentication.
It allows the application to display or use the authenticated user's information, such as name, email, and roles,
within the application context.

200.description: Indicates that the user information was successfully retrieved.

404.description: Indicates that the user could not be found.

put.summary: Updates the current user's information with the provided user model.
put.description:
This method allows authenticated users to update their profile details, such as name, email, and other personal
information.
It is crucial for maintaining up-to-date user information within the application.

requestBody.description:
An `OrganizationUserModel` object containing the updated user information.
The model should include all the necessary details that need to be updated in the user's profile.

200.description: Indicates that the user information was successfully updated.

404.description: Not Found

put.summary: Updates the current user's information with the provided user model.

This method allows authenticated users to update their profile details, such as name, email, and other personal
information.

requestBody.description:
An `OrganizationUserModel` object containing the updated user information.

200.description: Indicates that the user information was successfully updated.

404.description: Not Found

put.summary: Updates the role of a specified user within the organization.
put.description:
This method allows company administrators to modify user roles, thereby changing the permissions and access levels
of the user within the application.
It is a critical function for managing user access and ensuring that users have appropriate permissions based on
their responsibilities.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the user ID and the new role ID to be assigned to the user.
The role ID must correspond to an existing role within the organization, and the user ID must refer to an existing
user.

200.description: Indicates that the user's role was successfully updated.

403.description: Indicates that the operation is forbidden due to insufficient permissions.

put.summary: Retrieves a list of roles that the current user is allowed to assign to other users.
put.description:
This method is useful for understanding which roles a user can assign within the application.
It is typically used in user management interfaces to populate role selection options based on the current user's
permissions.

requestBody.description: The dto containing information about which user to update and what to set their active status to

200.description: Indicates that the list of assignable roles was successfully retrieved.
OK

get.description: GET /user/permissions

post.summary: Resends an invitation email to a user who has not yet confirmed their email address.
post.description:
This method is typically used by administrators to resend account setup or confirmation emails to users who may not
have received or acted upon the initial invitation.
It ensures that users can complete their account setup and gain access to the application.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the user to whom the invitation email will be resent.
This ID should correspond to a user in the system who has previously been invited but has not yet confirmed their
email address.

200.description: Indicates that the invitation has been successfully resent.

404.description: Indicates that the user could not be found.

409.description: Indicates that the user has already confirmed their email.

403.description: Indicates that the user does not have access to the organization.

post.summary: Invites a new user to the organization by sending them an email invitation.
post.description:
This method creates a new user entity in the system with the provided email address and assigns the specified role
and claims to the user.
It is primarily used by administrators to onboard new users into the organization, allowing them to access the
application with predefined permissions based on their role.

requestBody.description:
An npstoday_v2.API.Core.Configuration.DomainModels.Organizations.InviteModel object containing the details of the user to be invited.
This includes the user's email address, optional user ID, role, and any claims that should be associated with the
user.
If the user ID is not provided, a new GUID will be generated.
The role determines the user's level of access and permissions within the application.

200.description: Indicates that the user has been successfully added and an invitation email has been sent.

put.summary: Updates the password for the currently authenticated user.
put.description:
This endpoint allows the currently authenticated user to change their password. The user must provide their old
password for verification purposes and the new password they wish to set.

requestBody.description: The model containing the old and new passwords.

200.description: Indicates that the password was successfully updated.

400.description:
Indicates that the password update failed, such as due to an incorrect old password or a
validation error with the new password.

put.summary: Updates the password for the currently authenticated user.
put.description:
This endpoint allows the currently authenticated user to change their password. The user must provide their old
password for verification purposes and the new password they wish to set.

requestBody.description: The model containing the old and new passwords.

200.description: Indicates that the password was successfully updated.

400.description:
Indicates that the password update failed, such as due to an incorrect old password or a
validation error with the new password.

post.summary:
Processes an Excel file uploaded via an HTTP request to invite users by email to use Outlook within the
organization.
post.description:
This method reads an Excel file containing a list of email addresses, checks for existing users by these emails,
and performs actions based on the user's existence in the database.
For new users, it creates an account, assigns a predefined role for limited access, and sends an email invitation
to access Outlook.
For existing users, it ensures they have the necessary access and claims to use Outlook and sends an invitation if
needed.

200.description: Indicates that the users have been successfully invited and processed.

400.description: Indicates that the request data is invalid or the Excel file is not properly formatted.

get.summary: Authenticates the user with Microsoft login and retrieves a token for accessing the application.
get.description:
This method supports Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) authentication, allowing users to sign in with their
Microsoft accounts.
It facilitates seamless integration with Microsoft services, enabling users to leverage their existing credentials
for authentication, thereby enhancing security and user experience.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description:
An optional parameter specifying the organization ID that the user is attempting to access.
If provided, the method will validate the user's membership and permissions within the specified organization,
ensuring they have the appropriate access rights.
If not provided, the method will proceed with the authentication process without organization-specific validation.

200.description: Indicates that the user has been successfully authenticated and a token has been issued.

403.description:
Indicates that the user does not have permission to access the specified organization or the
authentication process failed.

get.summary: Creates a web token for the employee using Microsoft login.
get.description:
This method authenticates the user via Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) and generates a web token for accessing
the web client.
It checks the user's access to the platform and returns the token along with the list of organizations the user has
access to.
If the user does not have access to any organizations or if the token is null, a 403 (Forbidden) status code is
returned.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Optional organization ID for specifying the organization.

200.description: Indicates that the user has been successfully authenticated and a token has been issued.

403.description: Indicates that the user does not have access to the platform.

get.summary: Performs login using Microsoft Outlook credentials.
get.description:
This method allows users to log in using their Microsoft Outlook credentials. It extracts the bearer token from the
request headers,
attempts to find a local user associated with the email obtained from the token, and performs various checks
including user lockout status
and email confirmation. If the user passes all checks, an organization-specific login process is initiated.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description:
Optional. The organization ID to associate with the login session. If not provided, a default or
existing association may be used.

200.description: Login successful. The user is authenticated and associated with the specified organization.

401.description:
Unauthorized. The login attempt failed due to invalid credentials, missing Microsoft token, user
lockout, or other authorization issues.

post.summary: Token based login method

200.description: OK

401.description: Unauthorized

post.description: Token based login method

post.summary: Adds or updates the phone number for the current user.
post.description:
This endpoint allows the current authenticated user to add or update their phone number.
After the phone number is submitted, a verification message is sent to the provided number.
The operation supports cancellation.

requestBody.description: The model containing the phone number to be added or updated.

200.description: Indicates that the phone number verification message was successfully sent.

500.description:
Indicates that there was an internal server error, and the phone number verification message could
not be sent.

post.summary: Verifies the phone number for the current user.
post.description:
This method verifies the phone number by matching the provided verification code with the one sent to the user's
phone.
It requires the user to be authenticated and to provide both the phone number and the verification code.
If verification succeeds, the phone number is updated for the current user's account.

requestBody.description: The model containing the phone number and verification code.

200.description: Indicates that the phone number was successfully verified and updated.

400.description:
Indicates that the verification failed, possibly due to an incorrect code or other validation
error.

post.summary: Enables Two-Factor Authentication (TFA) for the current user.
post.description:
This endpoint allows the current authenticated user to enable Two-Factor Authentication (TFA) for their account.
Once enabled, the user will be required to provide a second form of authentication, in addition to their password,
upon logging in. This adds an additional layer of security to the user's account.

200.description: Indicates that TFA was successfully enabled for the user's account.

400.description: Indicates that the operation failed, possibly due to the user not being found or other issues.

post.summary: Disables Two-Factor Authentication (TFA) for the current user.
post.description:
This endpoint allows the current authenticated user to disable Two-Factor Authentication (TFA) for their account.
Disabling TFA removes the requirement for a second form of authentication upon logging in, which may decrease the
security of the user's account.
It is recommended to use this feature with caution.

200.description: Indicates that TFA was successfully disabled for the user's account.

400.description: Indicates that the operation failed, possibly due to the user not being found or other issues.

post.summary: Initiates the password recovery process for a user.
post.description:
This endpoint is called when a user clicks the "forgot password" option. It triggers the process to send password
recovery options to the user's registered email address.

requestBody.description:
The model containing the email address for which the password recovery process should be
initiated.

200.description:
Indicates that the password recovery process has been initiated. Note: This response is returned
regardless of whether the email is registered or not, for security reasons.

put.summary: Resets the password for a user based on a reset token.
put.description:
This endpoint is called when a user attempts to reset their password using a reset token they received via email.
It validates the model state, verifies the user's existence, and attempts to reset the password using the provided
token and new password.
If the password reset is successful and the user's email has not been confirmed, it will confirm the user's email
as well.

requestBody.description: The model containing the user ID, reset token, and new password.

200.description: Indicates that the password was successfully reset.

400.description: Indicates that the password reset attempt failed due to an invalid model state or reset token.

404.description: Indicates that the user was not found.

200.description: OK

get.description: GET /user/password-requirements

post.summary: Sets up a new user account using the information provided in the setup model.
post.description:
This endpoint is called when a user clicks the link in the setup account email. It configures the user's account
with the provided name and new password,
using the reset token to validate the request. This process is critical for new user onboarding, ensuring that only
the intended recipient of the setup email
can configure their account.

requestBody.description: The setup model containing the user's email, name, new password, and reset token.

200.description: Indicates that the account was successfully configured.

400.description:
Indicates that the account setup could not be completed, possibly due to an invalid link or
expired reset token.

404.description: Indicates that the user was not found.

post.summary: Validates a password reset token for a given user's email.
post.description:
This endpoint is used to validate the password reset token that a user receives via email when they initiate a
password reset process.
It checks if the provided token is valid for the specified email address. This is a crucial step in the password
reset flow to ensure
that the token used is legitimate and corresponds to the user's request.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address of the user to whom the token was sent.

requestBody.description: The password reset token to validate.

200.description:
Indicates that the token was successfully validated. The response body contains a boolean value
indicating the validity of the token.

get.summary: Retrieves the profile information for the currently authenticated user.
get.description:
This endpoint is used to fetch the profile details of the currently logged-in user. It attempts to find the user
based on the user ID obtained from the authentication token.
If the user is found, their profile information is returned. Otherwise, a NotFound response is generated.
This method is essential for displaying user-specific information on the client side.

200.description: Indicates that the user's profile was successfully retrieved.

404.description: Indicates that the user's profile could not be found.

post.summary: Retrieves the profile information for a user based on their email address.
post.description:
This endpoint is accessible only to users with CompanyAdmin role. It allows fetching the profile details of a user
by their email address.
The method checks if the requested user exists and if the current user has access to the same organization as the
requested user.
If the user does not exist or if there's no access to the user's organization, a NotFound response is returned.

requestBody.description: The email address of the user whose profile is being requested.

200.description: Indicates that the user's profile was successfully retrieved.

404.description:
Indicates that the user's profile could not be found or access to the user's organization is not
granted.

post.summary: Grants access to a user for an organization with a specified role.
post.description:
This endpoint allows CompanyAdmins and SuperAdmins to grant access to users for specific organizations.
CompanyAdmins can only grant access
to their own organization and cannot assign roles higher than their own. SuperAdmins can grant access to any
organization without restrictions.
The method checks if the input model is valid, identifies the user by email or ID, and then updates or adds the
user's access based on the provided information.

requestBody.description: The model containing the user's email or ID, organization ID, and the role to be granted.

200.description: Indicates that access has been successfully granted.

400.description:
Indicates that the request was invalid, either due to missing information or because the user does
not exist.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of organizations that the currently authenticated user has access to.
get.description:
This endpoint is used to fetch all the organizations that the current user can access. It queries the database for
organizations
associated with the user's ID and returns a list of these organizations. This is useful for applications that need
to display or
allow selection among multiple organizations that a user is a part of.

200.description: Indicates that the list of accessible organizations was successfully retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieves the claims associated with the currently authenticated user.
get.description:
This endpoint is used to obtain the claims of the currently logged-in user. Claims are key/value pairs associated
with a user that provide specific information such as user roles, permissions, and other custom data.
This method queries the database for all claims associated with the user's ID and returns them. It is useful for
client applications to understand the user's permissions and roles within the application.

200.description: Indicates that the claims were successfully retrieved.

post.summary: Adds claims to users within the organization.
post.description:
This endpoint allows CompanyAdmins to add claims to users within their organization, enhancing or defining their
access and permissions.
SuperAdmins have the ability to add claims to any user across all organizations without restrictions. The method
checks if the current user
is attempting to modify their own claims, which is not permitted. It also verifies if the current user has the
authority to add claims to the
specified users based on their organizational roles and the target users' association with the organization. Claims
are added only if all
validations pass.

requestBody.description: A list of claims to be added to users.

200.description: Indicates that the claims were successfully added.

400.description:
Indicates a bad request, such as attempting to add claims to oneself or lacking permission to add
claims to one or more users.

404.description: Indicates that one or more users to whom claims were to be added do not exist.

delete.summary: Deletes specified claims from users within the organization.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows CompanyAdmins to remove claims from users within their organization.
The method first checks if the current user is attempting to remove claims from themselves, which is not permitted.
It then verifies if the current user has
the authority to remove the specified claims based on their organizational roles and the target users' association
with the organization. Claims are removed only
if all validations pass. The method returns the number of claims successfully deleted.

requestBody.description: A list of claims to be deleted.

200.description: Indicates that the claims were successfully deleted.

400.description:
Indicates a bad request, such as attempting to remove claims from oneself or lacking permission to
remove claims from one or more users.

404.description: Indicates that the specified claims or users do not exist.

get.summary: Retrieves the claims associated with a specified user by their ID.
get.description:
This endpoint is accessible only to users with the CompanyAdmin role. It allows fetching the claims of a user
identified by their unique ID.
Claims are key/value pairs that represent attributes of the user, such as roles and permissions. This method checks
if the user exists and,
if so, returns all claims associated with that user. It's useful for managing user permissions and roles within an
organization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user whose claims are being requested.

200.description: Indicates that the claims were successfully retrieved.

404.description: Indicates that the specified user does not exist.

delete.summary: Deletes a specific claim from a user within the organization.
delete.description:
This endpoint allows CompanyAdmins to remove a specific claim from a user by claim ID. It first checks if the claim
exists and if the current user
has access to the organization associated with the claim. If the user does not have access or the claim does not
exist, the request is denied.
This is useful for revoking specific permissions or roles from a user within an organization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the claim to be deleted.

200.description: Indicates that the claim was successfully deleted.

400.description:
Indicates that the claim does not exist or the user does not have access to the organization
associated with the claim.

post.summary: Creates a new organization and confirms the email of the contact person.
post.description:
It allows for the creation of a new organization based on the provided model, which includes details such as the
organization's name, contact email, and contact password. The method also confirms the email of the contact person
as part of the organization creation process. This is crucial for ensuring that the contact person's email is
verified and can be used for further
communication and authentication purposes.

requestBody.description:
The model containing the necessary information to create a new organization and confirm the contact
email.

200.description: Indicates that the organization was successfully created and the contact email confirmed.

verified and can be used for further
communication and authentication purposes.

requestBody.description:
The model containing the necessary information to create a new organization and confirm the contact
email.

200.description: Indicates that the organization was successfully created and the contact email confirmed.

get.summary: Changes the current user's active organization to the specified organization.
get.description:
This endpoint allows a user to switch their active organization context to another organization they are a member
of.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization to switch to.

200.description: Indicates that the organization was successfully changed.

get.summary: Checks the operational status of the database to ensure it is up and running.
get.description:
This method serves as a health check endpoint used by monitoring services like Statuspal to verify the availability
and responsiveness of the database.
It's a critical part of maintaining high availability and operational reliability, allowing for immediate detection
and response to database downtimes.

200.description: Indicates that the database is operational.

503.description: Indicates that the database is not operational.

head.summary: Checks the operational status of the database to ensure it is up and running.
head.description:
This method serves as a health check endpoint used by monitoring services like Statuspal to verify the availability
and responsiveness of the database.
It's a critical part of maintaining high availability and operational reliability, allowing for immediate detection
and response to database downtimes.

200.description: Indicates that the database is operational.

503.description: Indicates that the database is not operational.

post.summary: Subscribes a new webhook with optional custom paths.
post.description:
This endpoint supports integration with various cloud services, allowing for flexible webhook configurations.
Some cloud services may necessitate unique endpoints for each trigger, which can be accommodated through the
optional subscribe path parameter.
Example Services:
1. Manual Setup WebHooks
2. Zapier
3. Power Automate (Custom Connectors)

parameters.name: optionalSubscribePath
parameters.description:
An optional parameter that allows specifying a custom path for the subscription. This is useful for integrating
with cloud services that require unique endpoints per trigger.
If not provided, a default subscription path is used.

requestBody.description:
The webhook model containing the subscription details. This includes the target URL, event types to subscribe to,
and other relevant information.

201.description: Indicates that the webhook was successfully created.

get.summary: Retrieves the details of a specific webhook subscription by its unique identifier.
get.description:
This method is essential for managing webhook subscriptions, allowing clients to verify the configuration and
status of a webhook.
It supports the functionality to fetch webhook details, which can be used for auditing, debugging, or displaying in
a user interface.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the webhook subscription to be retrieved. This ID is typically obtained at the time of
webhook creation or through a listing of existing webhooks.
It uniquely identifies the webhook subscription within the system.

200.description: Indicates that the webhook subscription was successfully retrieved.

404.description: Indicates that the webhook subscription was not found.

delete.summary: Unsubscribes a specific webhook subscription by its unique identifier.
delete.description:
This operation removes the webhook configuration and stops any future notifications from being sent to the
previously configured endpoint.
It is useful for managing webhook subscriptions dynamically, allowing users to remove webhooks that are no longer
needed or relevant.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the webhook subscription to be unsubscribed.
This ID should match the one used when the webhook was initially created or obtained through a listing of webhooks.
It uniquely identifies the webhook subscription within the system.

200.description: Indicates that the webhook subscription was successfully deleted.

404.description: Indicates that the webhook subscription was not found.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all webhook subscriptions for the current organization.
get.description:
This method is useful for managing multiple webhook subscriptions, allowing clients to list all configured webhooks
and their details.
It supports operations such as auditing, debugging, or displaying webhook configurations in a user interface.

200.description: Indicates that the list of webhook subscriptions was successfully retrieved.

delete.summary: Unsubscribes all webhook subscriptions for the current organization.
delete.description:
This operation removes all webhook configurations associated with the organization, effectively stopping all future
notifications.
It is useful for cleaning up webhook subscriptions in bulk, especially when reconfiguring or decommissioning
services.

200.description: Indicates that all webhook subscriptions were successfully deleted.

get.summary: Checks the operational status of a specified service provider within a predefined time window.
get.description:
This endpoint is utilized by Statuspal to verify if the underlying service providers (SMS or Email) are up and
running by checking their activity in the last 15 minutes.
It's crucial for monitoring the health and availability of services that are integral to application functionality.

parameters.name: providerType
parameters.description:
Specifies the type of the provider to check. Accepted values are "sms" for SMS service providers and "email" for
Email service providers.
This parameter determines which service's operational status will be checked.

parameters.name: providerName
parameters.description:
The name of the specific provider to check. This is an enumeration that identifies the provider uniquely, allowing
the system to query the appropriate service's status.

200.description: Indicates that the specified provider is operational.

400.description: Indicates that the provider is not operational or the providerType is not recognized.

404.description: Not Found

head.summary: Checks the operational status of a specified service provider within a predefined time window.
head.description:
This endpoint is utilized by Statuspal to verify if the underlying service providers (SMS or Email) are up and
running by checking their activity in the last 15 minutes.
It's crucial for monitoring the health and availability of services that are integral to application functionality.

200.description: Indicates that the specified provider is operational.

400.description: Indicates that the provider is not operational or the providerType is not recognized.

404.description: Not Found

post.summary: Tests the specified webhook URL by sending a predefined response model to it.
post.description:
This method is primarily used for validating the functionality and connectivity of a webhook endpoint.
It ensures that the endpoint is correctly configured to receive and process webhook calls from the system.
This can be particularly useful during the setup or troubleshooting of webhook integrations.

requestBody.description:
Dto containing information about the webhook to test. The URL should be the full endpoint address where webhook
payloads
are expected to be received.
It is important that this URL is accessible from the system sending the test payload.

200.description: Indicates that the webhook call was successfully received and processed.

400.description: Indicates that the webhook call failed due to invalid input or other issues.
